description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who is about to ship a very small,
    self-contained “sensor-collector” workload to a fleet of IoT devices.
    Before shipping, you must prove that the workload can be started,
    verified to be alive, and stopped again, *all from an unprivileged
    Linux shell session*.

    Deliverables (all must be placed in /home/user/iot_edge):

      1. /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector.sh  
         • Bash script that runs an **infinite** “while true” loop.  
         • Every second it must write one line to STDOUT in the
           exact format  
               TIMESTAMP=<iso-8601>,VALUE=<random-int-0-100>  
           Example:  
               TIMESTAMP=2024-05-10T14:40:01Z,VALUE=17  

      2. /home/user/iot_edge/data.log  
         • Output file that will receive the STDOUT stream produced by
           sensor_collector.sh while it is running in the background.  
         • It must end up containing **at least three** such lines.

      3. /home/user/iot_edge/collector.pid  
         • A single-line file containing only the PID of the
           running sensor_collector.sh process **without** a trailing
           newline.

      4. /home/user/iot_edge/process_log.txt  
         • MUST contain **exactly four** newline-separated lines,
           in this order, using UPPERCASE tags:  

               STARTED:<pid>  
               VERIFIED:<pid>  
               STOPPED:<pid>  
               COMPLETE  

           where <pid> is the **same** decimal PID written to
           collector.pid.  No extra spaces are allowed.

    Required workflow (high-level):

      • Create the directory structure.  
      • Write the sensor_collector.sh script and make it executable.  
      • Start the script **in the background** with stdout redirected
        (append mode is fine) to data.log.  
      • Capture its PID, store it in collector.pid, and append the
        “STARTED:<pid>” line to process_log.txt.  
      • Use a process-listing utility (e.g., ps) to confirm that the PID
        is running; upon success, append “VERIFIED:<pid>”.  
      • Gracefully stop the workload (SIGTERM is enough), wait until the
        process is fully terminated, then append “STOPPED:<pid>”.  
      • Finish by appending a final line “COMPLETE”.  

    Additional checks that the automated grader will perform:

      • sensor_collector.sh must have the executable bit set.  
      • data.log must contain ≥ 3 well-formed lines following the exact
        TIMESTAMP=…,VALUE=… pattern (regex will be used).  
      • collector.pid must contain only digits and match the PID used in
        all three tag lines of process_log.txt.  
      • process_log.txt must contain *exactly* four lines, no extra
        whitespace or blank lines.

    The entire task can be accomplished with roughly 5-10 standard
    Linux commands (mkdir, cat/echo, chmod, nohup/&, ps/pgrep, kill,
    etc.).  Do **NOT** run anything as root.  
    Once all deliverables are in place, the scenario is complete.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth information the grader will rely on -->
    Directory to exist:
      /home/user/iot_edge

    Files that must exist after completion:
      /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector.sh   (mode 0755)
      /home/user/iot_edge/data.log              (contains ≥3 lines,
                                                 each matching:
                                                 ^TIMESTAMP=.*?,VALUE=[0-9]{1,3}$)
      /home/user/iot_edge/collector.pid         (regex: ^[0-9]+$)
      /home/user/iot_edge/process_log.txt       (exactly 4 lines)
    
    Expected process_log.txt format:
        1st line => ^STARTED:([0-9]+)$
        2nd line => ^VERIFIED:\1$              # same captured PID
        3rd line => ^STOPPED:\1$
        4th line => ^COMPLETE$

    Additional validation steps the grader performs:

      • Reads PID from collector.pid → P.  
      • Ensures P appears in all three tag lines and nowhere else.  
      • Confirms that no process with PID P is running after STOPPED.  
      • Confirms that sensor_collector.sh produced at least three output
        lines in data.log before being stopped.  
      • Verifies sensor_collector.sh is executable (stat checks mode 0755
        or at least user-execute bit).

    No pre-existing files or processes are provided; the working
    directory /home/user/iot_edge does *not* exist at container start
    and must be created by the agent.
